text: Why don't they stop the clock after a goal? Can the other team throw the ball in and start play?
label: hot_take

text: Why don't they stop the clock after a goal? The clock keeps running until the referee blows his whistle for either half time (45 minutes plus stoppage time around 1-3 minutes, depending on how many stoppages in play there have been) or full time (another 45 minutes plus stoppage time, so 90 minutes in total plus stoppage time after each half). Can the other team throw the ball in and start play? No. After a goal, the ball is placed in the middle of the field, and the conceding team resumes play from the ref's whistle.
